title: On Iran, Trump Keeps World Off Balance With Ever-Changing Threats
summary: Global leaders are struggling in their efforts to find a way to end the American-Israeli war on Iran, and they are spooked about what President Trump might do next.

title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
summary: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

title: The Latest Blows to Iran’s Leadership
summary: An Iranian intelligence chief killed overnight on Monday was one of several Iranian officials who occupied their posts for only a few months.

title: Newly Obtained Video of Minneapolis Shooting Undermines ICE Account
summary: Prosecutors did not watch video of the shooting until weeks after charging the wounded man, an official said.

title: Plunging International Student Enrollment Under Trump Squeezes Colleges
summary: The Trump administration’s campaign to curtail international students is not just hitting the elite schools targeted by the government.

title: Trump’s USAID Overhaul Sent Millions More Dollars to Big U.S.-Based Contractors
summary: While organizations in the developing world were nearly shut out, the big aid agencies DOGE had called wasteful received huge infusions of cash, a new analysis found.

title: BNY and Robinhood Will Help Run ‘Trump Accounts’ for Children
summary: The new tax-sheltered savings and investment accounts will start accepting deposits this summer.

title: What Does an Ultra-Luxury First Class Ticket Get You? Flying in a Frictionless Bubble.
summary: A writer reports from inside the premium bubble, where there’s no such thing as too much, petty annoyances are nonexistent and the real world never intrudes.

title: In Savannah Guthrie’s Return to ‘Today,’ a Rare TV Example of How to Live With Not Knowing
summary: With her mother still missing, the “Today” host’s comeback was a rare TV example of learning to live with not knowing.

title: What We Know About the Kidnapping of Nancy Guthrie, Savannah Guthrie’s Mother
summary: Nancy Guthrie, 84, the “Today” show anchor’s mother, vanished from her Arizona home on Feb. 1. In the time since, very little new information has come to light.

title: The California Lake Billed as the ‘Saudi Arabia of Lithium’
summary: Residents of Imperial County, Calif., are in dire need of an economic boost. Experts say the answer lies beneath the Salton Sea, where a lithium trove sits.

title: Forget the A.I. Apocalypse. Memes Have Already Nuked Our Culture.
summary: From our jokes and slang to the White House’s policy messaging, internet “brain rot” has escaped our phones to take over … well, everything.

title: Epstein in Paris: How a Sex Offender Hustled for Access to France’s Elite
summary: Jeffrey Epstein spent his last days of freedom in Paris, meeting with influential figures. It was a playbook he used everywhere he lived to stamp a veneer of respectability on a life of sordid criminality.

title: Bard College Is Split Over Its President and His Epstein Ties
summary: Leon Botstein saved Bard from near ruin. Now, as an outside firm conducts a review, the campus is home to arguments about his legacy and future.

title: Ahead of 2026 Midterms, Republicans Unveil a $342 Million Battle Plan to Keep the Senate
summary: The main super PAC for Senate Republicans is focusing on eight states, and plans to spend big money to defend G.O.P.-held seats in Alaska, Iowa and Ohio.

title: The Big Bang: A.I. Has Created a Code Overload
summary: Companies are scrambling to deal with the glut.

title: A.I. Is on Its Way to Upending Cybersecurity
summary: With new systems from companies like Anthropic and OpenAI, hackers can attack with greater speed. The defense is more A.I.

title: San Francisco Sobers Up
summary: San Francisco gets its act together.

title: It’s Called Silicon Sampling, and It’s Going to Ruin Public Opinion Polling
summary: Instead of navigating the obstacles to conduct polls with human respondents, pollsters are running A.I. simulations instead. Why?

title: Sharpton Moves Longtime Civil Rights Group to New Home in Harlem
summary: The Rev. Al Sharpton wants to leave his mark in the face of gentrification, which he says has diluted Harlem’s political power.

title: A Hidden Russian Hand in Hungary’s Election? Actually, It’s Quite Open.
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has made hostility to Ukraine a centerpiece of his campaign. Moscow seems determined to repay the favor.

title: Trump’s Board of Peace Gives Hamas Disarmament Deadline
summary: The demand reflects both the U.S. administration’s eagerness to secure a lasting cease-fire in Gaza and its growing impatience with the Palestinian militant group.

title: Federal Agency Unveils Three Potential Osteoarthritis Treatments
summary: With funding from ARPA-H, three teams of researchers have regrown bone and cartilage, even entire knees, in animal studies. Human trials are not far off.
